title: 10 best herbal capsules for hangover

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with hangover because they contain potent antioxidants that neutralize toxins accumulated during excessive drinking. The capsules' extracts, including huperzine A and flavonoids, have been shown to effectively combat nausea, dizziness, and fatigue, common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, the antioxidants help to alleviate inflammation in the body, which can be triggered by alcohol consumption. By taking maidenhair tree capsules before or after drinking, individuals may experience reduced severity and duration of their hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with a hangover.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's active compounds can dilate blood vessels, potentially helping to alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache and migraines.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These terpenoids may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially helping to alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.

Bilobalide: This specific terpenoid may have neuroprotective properties, which could help mitigate the damage to brain cells caused by excessive alcohol consumption, leading to reduced severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used due to their potential to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant properties that may help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used due to their potential to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves when they are mature and have a deep green color during fall season.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Weigh 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of vegetable glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with hangover because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The capsules work by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress and unease often associated with hangovers. Additionally, maypop's antioxidant properties help to neutralize toxins and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and headaches, leaving you feeling refreshed and rejuvenated after a night out.
remedy_properties: Passionflower flavonoids: Help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate hangover symptoms, by binding to GABA receptors in the brain.
Papaverine alkaloids: Act as a vasodilator, which helps to alleviate headaches and improve blood flow, reducing the severity of hangover-related pain.

Vitamins and minerals (B-complex): Support the body's natural detoxification processes by replenishing essential nutrients that may be depleted due to heavy alcohol consumption, helping to reduce the overall effects of a hangover.
remedy_constituents: Isophorone: It has sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.

Harman alkaloids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms such as fatigue and muscle pain.

Flavonoids (e.g., vitexin and isovitexin): They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce hangover symptoms such as inflammation, nausea, and fatigue by protecting the body from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and sedative properties.
Fruits: Utilized for their rich content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms like fatigue and dehydration.
Barks: Employed for their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers at 50c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1:1 ratio of powder to vegetable cellulose powder to form a uniform blend.
Fill 00 capsules with 200mg of the blend and seal each capsule carefully with a capsule filling machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with hangover because they contain a powerful herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The herb, Scutellaria lateriflora, is known for its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. When taken in capsule form, skullcap helps to neutralize the toxins produced by excessive alcohol consumption, allowing the body to recover more quickly and reducing the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Scutellarein: A flavonoid compound found in skullcap that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Baicalin: A flavonoid glycoside that exhibits antioxidant activity, which can help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, potentially reducing the severity of hangover symptoms.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in skullcap, such as baicalein, have been shown to have a sedative effect, which may help to alleviate symptoms such as insomnia and irritability often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have antioxidant properties that can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive drinking, potentially reducing hangover symptoms.
Bacopasides: These alkaloid compounds have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and as a sedative, which could help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Baicalein: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea and stomach discomfort by protecting the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: They are used to create extracts with potential adaptogenic properties, which may help regulate and balance the body's response to stress and discomfort caused by hangovers.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, similar to the leaves, which may contribute to their potential hangover-relieving effects by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers and leaves for use in this recipe.
Dry the harvested material further for 2 hours at a temperature of 105 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 250 milliliters of glycerin to create a 1:5 ratio for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with hangover because they contain a natural compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with excessive drinking. The herb also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headaches and fatigue caused by dehydration. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, helping to ease the discomfort and irritability that often accompanies a hangover.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help to calm an overactive nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can be particularly helpful in alleviating hangover-induced stress and tension.

Antioxidant Properties: Valerian's antioxidant compounds can help to neutralize free radicals that are produced during heavy drinking, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as fatigue, nausea, and headaches.

Gastrointestinal Soothing Properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues that often occur after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: Helps with hangover by reducing inflammation and soothing the nervous system, alleviating headaches and anxiety.
Isovaleric Acid: Contributes to hangover relief by acting as a natural sedative, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of insomnia.
Valerenal: Assists in hangover recovery by exhibiting anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the mind and body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are primarily used to make hangover capsules due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which is believed to have a sedative effect.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are also utilized, as they contain some of the same bioactive compounds as the roots, although in lower concentrations.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are occasionally used, although less commonly, as they are said to have a milder effect compared to the roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Dry the valeriana officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the valeriana officinalis powder with 100 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with hangover because they contain potent natural ingredients that work to alleviate symptoms of a hangover. The herbs in these capsules, such as ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, help to soothe an upset stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and calm the mind and body. By replenishing essential vitamins and minerals lost during excessive drinking, herbal tea capsules can also provide a natural energy boost to get you back on your feet.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Helps to alleviate nausea and reduce inflammation, which can be especially helpful during a hangover when stomach discomfort and bloating are common symptoms.
Peppermint: Soothes digestive issues by relaxing the muscles in the gut and reducing spasms, which can help ease hangover-related stomach cramps and irritability.

Turmeric: Contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Catechins (Especially EGCG): These polyphenolic compounds help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and improving liver function.

Theaflavins and Thearubigins: These catechin-derived compounds have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

Caffeine: Although often considered a contributor to hangovers, caffeine in moderation (as found in green tea) may actually help alleviate headaches, improve alertness, and reduce fatigue associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tea, which can be beneficial for relaxation and hydration.
Stems: They can be used to make tea as well, although it's not as common as leaf tea.
Buds: They can be used to make tea, providing a slightly different flavor and nutritional profile compared to leaf tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of camellia sinensis leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 10g of the powdered camellia sinensis with 30g of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Fill 20 empty capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine at 100 capsules per minute.
Store the herbal camellia sinensis capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with hangover because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed before or after heavy drinking, these capsules may help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue by soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to calm stomach upset and alleviate congestion, making it a natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and nausea caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, a type of antioxidant, which can help neutralize free radicals generated during hangover, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall liver health.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have been shown to have anticonvulsant properties, which can help alleviate headaches and migraines commonly associated with hangovers.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides: These compounds, particularly glycyrrhizin, help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, stabilizing the stomach lining, and regulating cortisol levels to mitigate the effects of excessive alcohol consumption.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help counteract oxidative stress and inflammation caused by hangover, promoting a faster recovery.

Steroids (Saponins): Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory and anti-ulcerogenic properties, which aid in soothing the digestive tract and reducing nausea associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Barks: The barks are also used to make capsules for hangover relief, as they contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make capsules for hangover relief, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a trusted supplier or a well-known market.
Dry the harvested root in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to ensure moisture content is reduced.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of a natural binder such as gelatin and 50 milligrams of starch for 3 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with hangover because they contain a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken before or after consuming alcohol, this compound can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to stabilize blood sugar levels, which can become disrupted after heavy drinking. By balancing blood sugar and reducing inflammation, these capsules can provide relief from the uncomfortable aftermath of a night out.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue by reducing inflammation in the body.

Cinnamon's vasodilatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon capsules contain compounds that can help dilate blood vessels, which can improve circulation and reduce blood pressure, potentially alleviating hangover-related symptoms like dizziness and lightheadedness.

Antiemetic properties: The volatile oils present in Ceylon cinnamon have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting, making it a potential natural remedy for hangover-induced stomach upset and queasiness.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Cinnamic acid: As a phenolic compound, cinnamic acid has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach and may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers by reducing inflammation and protecting the gastric mucosa.

Eugenol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Barks: The inner bark of Cinnamomum verum is commonly used due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum contain compounds that aid in digestion and relieve nausea, making them a popular choice for hangover remedies.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome or underground stem of Cinnamomum verum is used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15g of cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees at dawn to ensure optimal oil content and flavor.
Dry the cinnamomum verum bark in a single layer at 40-50°c for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 2-3g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 5-7g of gelatin and 2-3g of water to form a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the cinnamomum verum mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the active ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with hangover because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to alleviate symptoms of hangover such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. It also helps to reduce inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to oxidative stress and damage to the liver and other organs. By taking herbal turmeric capsules after a night out, individuals can help their body recover from the negative effects of binge drinking.
remedy_properties: C Curcumin: A potent antioxidant compound found in turmeric, curcumin helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids and polyphenols, help alleviate hangover-induced pain, headache, and fatigue by reducing inflammation in the body.

Gastroprotective Properties: The mucilages and saponins present in turmeric capsules help soothe and protect the digestive tract from the irritating effects of alcohol, which can contribute to nausea, stomach upset, and other hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, may help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and pain associated with excessive alcohol consumption.
Volitin: Volitin, a sesquiterpene, may help mitigate hangover effects by exhibiting antioxidant properties that counteract oxidative damage caused by alcohol metabolism.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, may aid in hangover relief by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines that contribute to hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain a significant amount of curcumin, which can help reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea, headache, and fatigue.
Stems: Stems are not typically used to make capsules for hangover, however, they are sometimes used as a filler or to support the other active ingredients in a supplement.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh curcuma longa roots with at least 2-3 inches of stalks attached.
Clean and wash the roots in cold water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Dry the curcuma longa roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400-500 mg of the powder and seal them to complete the herbal capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with hangover because they contain natural compounds that alleviate nausea, stomach discomfort, and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger work to reduce oxidative stress and prevent damage to the digestive tract, while its natural acidity helps to neutralize the alkalinity of blood, reducing the severity of symptoms. Additionally, ginger's warming effect helps to increase blood circulation, which can help to replenish depleted nutrients and electrolytes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headache, and fatigue caused by a hangover.

Digestive enzymes: Ginger has natural digestive enzymes that can help stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and stomach discomfort often associated with excessive alcohol consumption.

Antioxidants: Ginger's high antioxidant content can help neutralize free radicals formed during alcohol metabolism, which can reduce oxidative stress and alleviate hangover symptoms like fatigue, dizziness, and brain fog.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols and Shogaols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.
Curcumin: As a phenolic compound, curcumin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce hangover-related inflammation and oxidative stress.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and nausea-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain active compounds that help in reducing nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of hangovers.
(Optional, but commonly used) Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale are also used to create supplements, although less common, they are still utilized in some hangover relief capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a clean knife, taking care not to bruise them.
Clean the roots with water, then dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill, taking about 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 1 gram of starch and 1 gram of gelatin to create a uniform blend, taking 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blend, making sure each capsule contains the correct dosage of 1 gram.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with hangover because they contain Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory that can alleviate symptoms of dehydration, such as headaches and fatigue. The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower also help to neutralize the toxins released by the liver during alcohol metabolism, reducing the severity of morning-after discomforts like nausea and dizziness. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's soothing properties may help to calm digestive upset and promote a sense of overall well-being after a night of indulgence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache and fatigue.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in coneflower, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help neutralize free radicals that accumulate during heavy drinking, potentially reducing oxidative stress and hangover severity.

Sobering and calming compounds: Coneflower contains a compound called cynarin, which has been traditionally used to reduce the effects of alcohol intoxication, helping to calm the stomach and ease hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea caused by inflammation in the body.
Alkaloids (Cichorine and Angustifolin): As natural antispasmodics, these alkaloids may help relax muscles, reduce cramping, and ease digestive discomfort that often accompany hangovers.

Phenolic Acids (Chlorogenic and Caffeic Acids): As antioxidants, these compounds can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like fatigue and brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used due to their high content of alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their potential to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their high content of caffeic acid derivatives, which may have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia from a reputable supplier within 2 years of harvest.
Dry the roots further at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill at 10000 rpm.
Mix 1g of the powder with 1g of gelatin and 1ml of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 50 empty capsules with 1g each of the blend and seal immediately using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with hangover because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The herbs in dandelion capsules may also help to replenish lost electrolytes and balance the body's pH levels, which can be disrupted by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, dandelion is known for its diuretic properties, which can help to flush out toxins from the liver and kidneys, further reducing hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion helps to increase urine production, which can help to flush out excess toxins and electrolytes lost during heavy drinking, reducing dehydration and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption, which may contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Liver-supportive compounds: Dandelion root and leaves contain compounds that have been traditionally used to support liver function, which can help the body detoxify alcohol more efficiently and reduce hangover severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that aid in reducing liver inflammation and detoxifying the body after excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered dandelion root with 250 milligrams of vitamin b12 and 100 milligrams of ginger powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container for up to six months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with hangover because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate headaches and nausea associated with hangovers. The calming effects of chamomile also help regulate digestion, which can ease stomach upset and discomfort caused by excessive drinking. Overall, herbal chamomile capsules provide a natural way to soothe the symptoms of a hangover and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing damage to cells and tissues and alleviating hangover symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate headaches, nausea, and fatigue commonly experienced during a hangover.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and apigenin work as a natural relaxant, calming the nervous system and promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness, which can help reduce anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, the roots help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes and coumarins, the seeds aid in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry matricaria chamomilla flowers for 24 hours at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind dried flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a decoction.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 15 minutes then cool.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled decoction and seal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with hangover because they contain a potent blend of herbs that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of beach rose help to reduce nausea and vomiting, while its antimicrobial properties combat the bacteria that can exacerbate hangover symptoms. Additionally, beach rose's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals caused by excessive drinking, promoting overall detoxification and replenishment of the body. As a result, taking beach rose capsules after a night out can provide quick relief from hangover woes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Beach Rose contains high amounts of antioxidants, which can help counteract oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption and reduce inflammation in the body.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate headaches, nausea, and other symptoms associated with hangover.

Digestive soothing properties: Beach Rose has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including indigestion, bloating, and stomach upset, which are common aftereffects of overindulgence in alcohol.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that aid in digestion and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh rosa rugosa petals at dawn or dusk for optimal potency.
Dry the petals in a warm and dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy and brittle.
Grind the dried petals into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground rosa rugosa with 2 parts cornstarch and fill empty #0 capsules by spooning.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with hangover because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that can help reduce inflammation and protect the liver from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption. When taken before or after drinking, milk thistle capsules may help mitigate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue associated with hangovers. Additionally, silymarin has been shown to promote detoxification and improve overall liver function, making it a natural remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as nausea and fatigue.

Flavonoids: Milk thistle's flavonoids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help neutralize free radicals generated during excessive drinking, thereby reducing oxidative stress and alleviating hangover symptoms like headache and dizziness.

Lectins: Silymarin's lectins have been found to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to the development of hangover symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain by activating the immune system and promoting inflammation in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps protect the liver and reduce inflammation, making it effective in alleviating hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and discomfort caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers from a clean and organic source.
Dry the plant material using a low-temperature oven or dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the silybum marianum powder with 500ml of distilled water to create a herbal extract, steeping for 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal extract solution and store in an airtight container at room temperature until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with hangover because it is an ancient Indian herb that has been used for centuries to promote mental clarity, memory, and cognitive function. Its active compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with hangovers. Additionally, brahmi's ability to enhance blood flow and oxygenation to the brain can help reduce symptoms of foggy thinking, dizziness, and irritability. By addressing these physical and mental effects, brahmi capsules provide a natural and effective way to recover from a night out.
remedy_properties: Cerebroprotective Properties: Brahmi's cerebroprotective properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache and fatigue.

Antioxidant Activity: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals caused by alcohol consumption, reducing damage to cells and tissues and alleviating hangover symptoms like nausea and stomach upset.

Nootropic Effects: Brahmi's nootropic effects can improve cognitive function, memory, and mental clarity, which may help counteract the impairment in coordination, concentration, and decision-making that often accompanies a hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and alkaloids, which help to detoxify the body and alleviate symptoms of hangover such as headaches and fatigue.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce nausea and stomach upset caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried bacopa monnieri powder into a clean glass bowl.
Mix the powder with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan over low heat.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 70 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before straining through a cheesecloth into capsules.
Fill 50 empty vegetarian capsules with the cooled bacopa monnieri extract and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil capsules helps with hangover because they contain compounds that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms of a night out. The essential oils in basil, such as eugenol and linalool, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce headaches and fatigue. Additionally, basil's antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacteria that can cause nausea and stomach upset after a heavy drinking session. By taking herbal basil capsules before or during a night out, you may find that your hangover symptoms are significantly reduced, allowing you to enjoy the morning after with renewed energy and clarity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and nausea commonly associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant properties: Basil's antioxidant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing the risk of damage to cells and tissues that can exacerbate hangover symptoms.

Gastric soothing properties: Basil has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the basil plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
Barks: Basil tree barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including those caused by excessive drinking, making it a suitable ingredient for hangover remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh basil leaves ocimum basilicum by cutting them from mature plants at dawn or dusk for maximum potency.
Dry the basil leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or until crumbly and brittle.
Grind the dried basil leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to release their active compounds.
Mix the powdered basil with 200mg of gelatin capsules and fill the capsules by hand or use an automatic encapsulator for consistent dosing.
Store the herbal ocimum basilicum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with hangover because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The antioxidants in blueberries can also help to neutralize free radicals produced by excess alcohol consumption, which can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the body's tissues. Additionally, the flavonoids present in blueberry extract have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with a hangover.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Blueberries contain powerful antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, alleviating symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Herbal blueberry capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like anthocyanins, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body, reducing hangover symptoms like pain, stiffness, and fatigue.

Digestive Soothing Compounds: Blueberries contain mucilages, such as pectin, that can help to soothe and calm an irritated digestive system, alleviating symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea often associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blueberry roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and headaches associated with hangovers.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate symptoms such as stomach upset and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality vaccinium myrtillus powder from reputable suppliers weighing 200g.
Mix the powder with 100g of gelatin in a large bowl stirring thoroughly for 10 minutes.
Add 20ml of distilled water gradually heating the mixture while stirring until gelatin dissolves fully.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the prepared gelatin mixture and seal them immediately taking approximately 30 minutes.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules help with hangover because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate oxidative stress in the body. After a night of heavy drinking, the liver is burdened with processing excess alcohol, leading to inflammation and damage. St John's Wort's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the liver and restore balance, reducing symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and nausea associated with hangovers. Additionally, its antioxidant effects may also help neutralize the negative effects of free radicals caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, contributing to hangover relief.
Hypericin: This flavonoid has been shown to have a sedative effect, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and irritability commonly associated with hangovers.

Xanthones: These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe stomach discomfort, nausea, and vomiting often experienced during hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that helps alleviate symptoms such as nausea and stomach pain commonly associated with hangovers.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried st john's wort flowers containing 0.3% hypericin for optimal efficacy.
Grind the dried st john's wort flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered st john's wort with 250 milliliters of distilled water to create a concentrated tincture.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with approximately 200 milligrams each of the st john's wort tincture, allowing for precise dosing and easy consumption.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year, ensuring freshness and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with hangover because they contain compounds that alleviate symptoms of nausea, bloating, and stomach discomfort commonly associated with excessive alcohol consumption. The capsules' natural antispasmodic properties help relax digestive muscles, reducing cramps and spasms that often occur after a night of heavy drinking. Additionally, fennel's carminative effects help eliminate gas and reduce bloating, allowing for quicker recovery and relief from hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Fennel's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to alleviate headache, fatigue, and nausea associated with hangovers.
Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to ease digestive discomfort, bloating, and gas that can occur after heavy drinking by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and flavonoids, which help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation caused by excessive drinking.
Seeds: Containing a natural compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps alleviate nausea and stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from a trusted source or grow your own.
Dry the harvested material in a warm well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 100 milliliters of cold distilled water to create a concentrated tincture, stirring well for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared tincture, using one half teaspoon per capsule and sealing immediately to preserve potency.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hangover are natural supplements designed to alleviate symptoms associated with excessive alcohol consumption. They typically combine various herbs, such as prickly pear cactus, ginger, and milk thistle, which work together to combat dehydration, nausea, and headaches. For instance, Morning Recovery Capsules contain a blend of adaptogenic herbs like Reishi mushroom and Ashwagandha that help regulate the body's natural detoxification processes, while others like Hangover Fix use a proprietary blend of herbal extracts to reduce hangover symptoms. By taking these capsules after a night out, individuals can significantly improve their quality of life by reducing discomfort, increasing energy levels, and allowing them to tackle their daily tasks with renewed vitality.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal capsules are migraines, sinusitis, and menstrual cramps. Herbal supplements containing ingredients such as feverfew, ginger, and willow bark can help alleviate migraine symptoms. Capsules made from herbs like eucalyptus and peppermint can soothe sinus pressure and congestion. Meanwhile, supplements containing dong quai and black cohosh have been traditionally used to ease menstrual discomfort. These herbal remedies offer a natural alternative to conventional treatments for these common ailments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with hangover is a blend of Milk Thistle, Dandelion, and Ginger. Milk Thistle contains silymarin, which aids in liver regeneration and detoxification, while Dandelion root helps to flush out toxins and reduce water retention. Ginger, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort. This synergistic combination supports the body's natural detoxification processes, promoting a faster recovery from hangover symptoms. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements.
